description:
You are a data-analyst who wants a tiny, one-shot diagnostic snapshot of the machine before you begin crunching large CSV datasets. Create a directory: /home/user/diagnostics Inside that directory produce exactly two files: 1. system_snapshot.csv • It must contain **exactly two lines**. • Line 1 (the header) must be **verbatim**: 
timestamp,hostname,uptime_seconds,total_mem_kb,free_mem_kb,load1,load5,load15
(all lower-case, single commas, no spaces before/after commas, and ending with a single newline). • Line 2 (the data row) must hold one snapshot of current system statistics, in the same order as the header; fields separated by single commas, no surrounding spaces, and terminated by **one newline**. ‑ timestamp: output of date +%s (UNIX epoch seconds, digits only). ‑ hostname: output of hostname. ‑ uptime_seconds: first value from /proc/uptime (may be integer or decimal). ‑ total_mem_kb and free_mem_kb: numeric values (only the number) from the lines “MemTotal” and “MemFree” in /proc/meminfo. ‑ load1, load5, load15: the three floating-point numbers shown in /proc/loadavg. Example structure (your numbers will differ): 
timestamp,hostname,uptime_seconds,total_mem_kb,free_mem_kb,load1,load5,load15
1686935012,myhost,12345.67,16340624,1213348,0.08,0.12,0.15
2. README.txt This file must contain **exactly two lines** (each ending with a newline): Line 1: system_snapshot.csv generated Line 2: /home/user/diagnostics/system_snapshot.csv Permissions: make both files world-readable (e.g. chmod 644). Do not create any additional files. You will only need a small handful of standard shell commands (≤ 3) to gather the data and write the files.
truth:
Expected artefacts for verification:

1. Directory /home/user/diagnostics exists.

2. /home/user/diagnostics/system_snapshot.csv  
   • mode 0644 (-rw-r--r--).  
   • first line identical to:  
     timestamp,hostname,uptime_seconds,total_mem_kb,free_mem_kb,load1,load5,load15\n  
   • file consists of exactly 2 lines.  
   • second (data) line has 8 comma-separated fields, matching:  
     Field 1: ^[0-9]+$ (epoch seconds)  
     Field 2: ^[A-Za-z0-9._-]+$ (hostname)  
     Field 3: ^[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)?$ (uptime)  
     Field 4 & 5: ^[0-9]+$  
     Field 6-8: ^[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)?$  

3. /home/user/diagnostics/README.txt  
   • mode 0644.  
   • contains exactly:  
     Line 1 = "system_snapshot.csv generated\n"  
     Line 2 = "/home/user/diagnostics/system_snapshot.csv\n"  

No further lines, no extra whitespace, no other files in /home/user/diagnostics.
name: task_000000_f7284f95
